title: cistus polymorphus var. lasiocalyx
intro: Cistus polymorphus var. lasiocalyx, also known as the rockrose, is a type of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae, characterized by its pink or white flowers and fragrant leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of rockrose include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions. Rockrose can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in perfumery and traditional folk medicine.
